rationale: The flooding originates from the policyholder's own plumbing (burst supply line), damaging the policyholder's real property (basement floor, drywall, and contents), which is a direct fit for property_damage coverage.
claim_summary: Sam Patel (POL-1004) reports basement flooding from a burst water supply line under the upstairs bathroom sink. Damage includes approximately two inches of water across the full basement, buckled hardwood flooring, soaked drywall to one foot height, and ruined stored boxes. Policy is active with $750,000 coverage and $500 deductible.

case_facts.incident_description: Basement flooded with approximately two inches of water across entire basement
case_facts.damage_details: Hardwood floor buckled, drywall soaked up to one foot high, stored boxes ruined
case_facts.cause: Burst water supply line under upstairs bathroom sink (policyholder's own plumbing)
case_facts.incident_date: Date discovered upon return from work trip (specific date to be confirmed)

rationale: Wind-driven damage to the policyholder's own roof structure (shingles on back porch overhang) is a straightforward property damage claim.
claim_summary: Homeowner Jordan Lee reports four shingles blown off the back porch overhang by wind last weekend. Estimated repair cost is $400 with no interior water damage or secondary damage. Given the $2,500 deductible on the policy, the claim may not result in a payout, but it will be reviewed by the property damage adjuster.

case_facts.description: four shingles blown off; no interior water damage
